::: notes
An *odds ratio* is a *ratio of odds*.
An odds is a ratio of probabilities,
so odds ratios are ratios of ratios:
:::

:::{#thm-or-ratio-ratio}
#### Odds ratio as a ratio of ratios

$$
\ba
\ratio(\odds_1, \odds_2)
&= \frac{\odds_1}{\odds_2}
\\
&= \frac{\paren{\frac{\pi_1}{1-\pi_1}}}{\paren{\frac{\pi_2}{1-\pi_2}}}
\ea
$$
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

::: proof
$$
\ba
\ratio(\odds_1, \odds_2)
&\eqdef \frac{\odds_1}{\odds_2}
&& \text{(@def-OR)}
\\ &= \frac{\paren{\frac{\pi_1}{1-\pi_1}}}{\paren{\frac{\pi_2}{1-\pi_2}}}
&& \text{(@thm-prob-to-odds, applied to each odds)}
\ea
$$
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exm-or-ratio-ratio}

#### The OC-MI odds ratio as a ratio of ratios

Using the MI risk estimates from @sol-compute-probs,
$\hat\pi(OC) = 13/5000$ and $\hat\pi(\neg OC) = 7/10000$,
@thm-or-ratio-ratio expresses the odds ratio
as a ratio of the two odds,
each of which is itself a ratio of probabilities:

```{r}
#| label: or-ratio-ratio-numeric
#| include: false
pi_oc <- 13 / 5000 # nolint: object_name_linter
pi_noc <- 7 / 10000 # nolint: object_name_linter
odds_oc <- pi_oc / (1 - pi_oc) # nolint: object_name_linter
odds_noc <- pi_noc / (1 - pi_noc) # nolint: object_name_linter
or_oc_noc <- odds_oc / odds_noc # nolint: object_name_linter
```

$$
\ba
\ratio(\odds(OC), \odds(\neg OC))
&\eqdef \frac{\odds(OC)}{\odds(\neg OC)}
&& \text{(@def-OR)}
\\ &= \frac{\paren{\frac{\pi(OC)}{1-\pi(OC)}}}{\paren{\frac{\pi(\neg OC)}{1-\pi(\neg OC)}}}
&& \text{(@thm-prob-to-odds, applied to each odds)}
\\ &= \frac{\paren{\frac{13/5000}{1 - 13/5000}}}{\paren{\frac{7/10000}{1 - 7/10000}}}
&& \text{(substituting the risk estimates)}
\\ &= \frac{`r odds_oc`}{`r odds_noc`}
&& \text{(computing each odds)}
\\ &= `r or_oc_noc`
&& \text{(dividing)}
\ea
$$

:::
